text.title: Product Operations Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding Product Operations plus the universal skip/run routing pair (referenced by the decision tree).

rule.statement: Product Ops MUST own roadmap tool, experiment platform, feedback ingestion, and metric warehouse plumbing; PMs MUST NOT be on the on-call rota for these systems.
rule.rationale: PMs maintaining their own tooling drift into ops; the explicit handoff is the only structural defence of discovery time.

rule.statement: There MUST be exactly one weekly product-metric rollup (single artefact, single owner, single distribution list); parallel rollups MUST be merged or killed.
rule.rationale: Parallel rollups produce divergent numbers and stakeholder confusion; canonical rollup is the only stable input to QBRs and strategy memos.

rule.statement: Stakeholder updates MUST use the fixed template (north-star metric, weekly delta, top-3 wins, top-3 risks, asks); ad-hoc-shape updates are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Variable-shape updates force stakeholders to re-learn each week and degrade signal-to-noise; the fixed template is the contract.

rule.statement: Every product process (intake, prioritisation, release, postmortem) MUST have exactly one canonical doc in .aidocs/process/; duplicates in Notion/Confluence MUST be marked as mirrors and redirect.
rule.rationale: Process docs in multiple locations diverge; the canonical-location rule plus mirror policy is the only durable single source.

rule.statement: Product Ops MUST attempt to automate a process before requesting headcount; the automation attempt and result MUST be recorded in the request.
rule.rationale: Heading-down-on-headcount-first is the most common cause of Ops bloat; the automation-first rule keeps the function lean.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `## Applies If` do not ALL hold OR any disqualifier in `## Skip If` fires, the agent MUST skip this methodology and document the skip reason in the cycle artefact.
rule.rationale: A minimum-form decision tree must route 'preconditions-not-satisfied' to skip; this rule is the canonical target.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `## Applies If` ALL hold AND no disqualifier fires, the agent MUST run the methodology end-to-end and produce the artefact specified in 02-output-contract.xml.
rule.rationale: Pair to skip-this-methodology; together they form the always-present minimal route.
